automatedVerification.notes: Local automation and the public sign-in smoke pass. This is not evidence for managed production accounts, the production deployment, or mobile viewport behavior.

checks.criterion: DM can create an account and sign in.
checks.notes: The managed-auth contract and preview build pass. A fresh production account confirmation and sign-in have not been recorded.

checks.criterion: Player can create an account and sign in.
checks.notes: A second real account must complete confirmation and sign-in independently of the DM account.
Automated repository validation passes. Production persistence must be confirmed with the real DM account.

checks.criterion: DM can invite a player with a shareable link.
checks.notes: Invite generation, hashing, expiry, rotation, and return-path contracts exist. A production link has not been exercised in this assessment.

checks.criterion: Player can join and access the campaign.
checks.notes: The invite acceptance and membership boundaries are validated in code. A separate production player session must prove access.

checks.criterion: DM can create and reopen a session.
checks.notes: Session create/update repository behavior is validated. The production UI journey remains to be recorded.

checks.criterion: Notes can be created, edited, saved, and recovered after reload.
checks.notes: Transactional note persistence and fallback parsing are covered. A live save/reload marker test and first-session usability sign-off remain.

checks.criterion: The character creation or companion path is usable for first players.
checks.notes: Character summaries support notes and retrieval, but there is no player-facing character route, navigation item, creation flow, or companion screen.

checks.criterion: Main DM and player flows work at a mobile viewport.
checks.notes: Responsive shell contracts exist. Auth, invite, campaign, session, and note flows still require a 390x844 production walkthrough.

checks.criterion: The production deployment is healthy before the test session.
checks.notes: Deployment safeguards and health checks are merged. A fresh production readiness workflow run against the intended session URL is required.

checks.criterion: Known issues and acceptable limitations are documented.
checks.notes: The runbook separates release blockers from accepted first-test limitations.

checks.criterion: A go or no-go decision is recorded.
checks.notes: Decision: NO-GO until every blocking check is cleared and the protected readiness workflow passes.

knownLimitations.summary: No player-facing character companion or character creation path exists.
Live note save/reload and at-table usability have not received first-test sign-off.
Production SMTP, confirmation delivery, and two-account authentication require a live walkthrough.
Only one active invite per campaign is supported; rotating a link revokes the previous link.
Local test identities are not migrated automatically to managed production accounts.
Advanced AI, rules automation, broad redesign, and performance benchmarking remain outside the first-test gate.
